rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular fluoroarylation of allyl aryl ethers to 3-fluorochromanes via an anodically generated difluoro-aryl-λ3-iodane mediator (ex-cell protocol)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (2e-) of aryl iodide (tolyl iodide, 2) in HF:amine/CH2Cl2 generates difluoro-λ3-aryl iodane 4; cathode reduces protons to H2. The iodane (generated ex-cell) then activates the pendant alkene of substrate 1 toward intramolecular Friedel-Crafts-type cyclization with fluoride delivery.

key_intermediates.name: iodonium-activated alkene adduct (5) from alkene + ArIF2
arene radical cation (from electron-rich substrate 1r, off-pathway SET)

electron_flow: At the anode, ArI (2) is oxidized by 2e- (losing nothing initially; ligand exchange with fluoride from HF:amine) to give ArIF2 (4); protons are reduced at the cathode to H2. ArIF2 then electrophilically activates the alkene of 1 to form iodonium intermediate 5, which undergoes intramolecular SEAr by the tethered aryl ether to give arenium 6; rearomatization with loss of H+ and fluoride delivery (with extrusion of ArI) furnishes 3-fluorochromane 3.
rate_determining_step: Intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the aromatic ring onto the iodane-activated alkene (intermediate 5); this step is outcompeted by fluoride attack (giving difluorination 7) when the arene is electron-poor (σp+m > 0.75) and is bypassed by direct SET on the arene when σp+m ≤ 0.

probe_reagents.name: iodotoluene (2)
probe_reagents.observation: CV in 5.6 HF:amine/DCM shows two oxidation features (peak currents linear with v^0.5) assigned to sequential 1e- oxidations at iodine to form ArIF2 (4); peaks merge at low [HF]

probe_reagents.name: electron-rich substrate 1r (no EWG on arene)
probe_reagents.observation: Solution turns deep blue on addition of 4; UV/vis red-shift matches that produced by NOBF4, indicating SET to give an arene radical cation; oxidation peak of 1r disappears upon addition of NOBF4, PIFA, or 4 — consistent with SET deactivating the arene

probe_reagents.name: NOBF4
probe_reagents.observation: Single-electron oxidant that reproduces the UV/vis red shift seen with 4, supporting SET pathway for electron-rich arenes

probe_reagents.name: PIFA (PhI(OCOCF3)2)
probe_reagents.observation: Consumes the oxidation feature of 1r in CV, confirming arene oxidation as the competing pathway

probe_reagents.name: Hammett series (substrate scope with varied σp+m)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of fluoroarylation 3 is high for 0 < σp+m < 0.75; difluorination 7 dominates for σp+m > 0.75; SET/no reaction for σp+m ≤ 0 — correlates product distribution with Epa/2 (1.2–1.8 V optimal)

probe_reagents.name: internal alkene substrate 1p
probe_reagents.observation: Largely unreactive, consistent with iodane activation of terminal/β-substituted alkenes

probe_reagents.name: homoallyl substrate 1q
probe_reagents.observation: Gives 6-exo product preferentially over 7-endo, consistent with cation/iodonium-controlled cyclization regiochemistry

notes: Two competing off-pathway regimes are explicitly mapped: (i) for very electron-poor arenes (σp+m > 0.75), intermolecular fluoride attack on iodonium 5 outcompetes intramolecular SEAr, giving 1,2-difluoride 7; (ii) for very electron-rich arenes (σp+m ≤ 0), direct SET from the arene to 4 generates an unreactive arene radical cation. An 'ex-cell' protocol (pre-generate 4, then add substrate) is essential — in-cell and divided-cell variants give <5% product, attributed to substrate/intermediate decomposition under direct electrolysis. No KIE, radical trap (TEMPO/BHT), or DFT data reported on these pages.
